title: fix(lock): Fix LockError message when releasing a lock.
body:
Fix #3535 

### Pull Request check-list

_Please make sure to review and check all of these items:_

- [ ] Do tests and lints pass with this change?
- [ ] Do the CI tests pass with this change (enable it first in your forked repo and wait for the github action build to finish)?
- [ ] Is the new or changed code fully tested?
- [ ] Is a documentation update included (if this change modifies existing APIs, or introduces new ones)?
- [ ] Is there an example added to the examples folder (if applicable)?
- [ ] Was the change added to CHANGES file?

_NOTE: these things are not required to open a PR and can be done
afterwards / while the PR is open._

### Description of change

We can get expect result for the test code in #3535  with this implementation:
```
Thread 2: Lock error: Cannot release a lock that's no longer owned
```

step_plan.summary: Ensure LockError/LockNotOwnedError exceptions stringify to their intended human-readable messages by properly initializing the base Exception with the message. Add sync and asyncio regression assertions so releasing a lock that is no longer owned prints 'Cannot release a lock that's no longer owned' as expected in #3535.

steps.operation: Fix LockError message propagation by initializing the base Exception with the provided message (and preserving existing attributes like lock_name) so str(exc) renders the human-readable text
steps.reason: Lock release paths already raise LockNotOwnedError with the correct message, but if LockError does not populate Exception.args (or otherwise define stringification), logging/printing the exception may omit the intended message. Ensuring super().__init__(message) (or setting args) makes str(e) include the message expected by #3535.
steps.side_effects: Changes the stringification/args of LockError and its subclasses; downstream code that inspects exc.args may now see the message where it was previously empty. Exception types and raising sites remain unchanged; run full test suite to ensure no assertions depend on the previous args behavior.

steps.operation: Add/adjust the sync lock regression test to assert the exception message text for the 'release when no longer owned' scenario using str(excinfo.value) (and keep asserting the exception type)
steps.reason: Provides a targeted regression for #3535 by verifying that the thrown LockNotOwnedError not only occurs, but also stringifies to 'Cannot release a lock that's no longer owned' when surfaced to users.
steps.side_effects: Test becomes stricter about the exact message; future message changes will require updating this assertion. May reveal other code paths where message propagation is inconsistent.

steps.operation: Add/adjust the asyncio lock regression test to assert the same exception message text for parity with sync behavior in the 'release when no longer owned' scenario
steps.reason: Async lock code should present identical exception messages to sync locks; asserting the same string output prevents regressions that only affect asyncio users.
steps.side_effects: Async tests become stricter about message content; may require aligning any async-only exception wrapping/raising to keep the message unchanged. Ensure CI runs asyncio tests in the same environments as sync tests.
